title: Containers created with docker-compose are not able to connect to each other by using their name
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

Creating multiple containers with a single docker-compose.yml should allow the running containers to talk to each other using the container name. Connecting via IP address works fine.

docker-compose.yml:

```
version: '3.3'

services:
    db:
        container_name: podman_compose_db
        image: mysql:5.6
        volumes:
            - db-volume:/var/lib/mysql
        ports:
            - "3306:3306"
        environment:
          MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD: root
    phpmyadmin:
        container_name: podman_compose_phpmyadmin
        image: phpmyadmin/phpmyadmin:latest
          ports:
          - "80:80"
        environment:
          - PMA_ARBITRARY=1

volumes:
  db-volume: 
```

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Run `sudo docker-compose -H unix:///run/podman/podman.sock up -d` 
2. Open `http://localhost/`
3. Try to connect to the database with host `podman_compose_db`, user `root` and password `root`

**Describe the results you received:**

PHPMyAdmin is not able to connect to the database

![grafik](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/159784/107420876-afa34780-6b19-11eb-9269-b002f44f4501.png)

**Describe the results you expected:**

PHPMyAdmin is able to connect to the database

**Output of `podman version`:**

```

Go Version:   go1.15.6
Git Commit:   de5f52893ee05851244b042e348ea3413b0c464d

OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

comments.body:
this worked perfectly for me ... you likely do not have the dnsname plugin installed.

```
sudo dnf install podman-plugins
```

This is likely not being built or dep'd by the COPR repository.
@baude Good catch, the dnsname plugin was not installed. Establishing a connection works after installing it and recreating the network
My containers are unable to communicate even with podman 3.2.3 and dns plugins installed on RHEL 8.4
Please file a new issue (or, even better, a Bugzilla)

In my case, I had to first install `dnf` into `podman machine` then install `podman-plugins`:

```bash
$> podman machine ssh sudo rpm-ostree install dnf
$> podman machine ssh sudo systemctl reboot 
$> podman machine ssh sudo dnf install podman-plugins
```

> this worked perfectly for me ... you likely do not have the dnsname plugin installed.
> 
> ```
> sudo dnf install podman-plugins
> ```
> 
> This is likely not being built or dep'd by the COPR repository.

Spent almost a month on this issue with Podman on RHEL. There is no notice or indication that I was missing a package that that would lack communication. Just end up with nginx spitting out the error:

`nginx: [emerg] host not found in upstream`

**Now it works like it should again**.🎉

FWIW, as mentioned by @d3xter, I had to recreate the network after installing `podman-plugins`. In other words, the network's `dns_enabled` setting needs to be set to `true`. In my case, before installing `podman-plugins`, `dns_enable` was set to `false` and after installing it, it was still set to `false`, which is why removing and recreating the network was needed.
😀
